name: Cell Source, Inc.
business: Cell Source, Inc. is a cell therapy company focused on immunotherapy, developing proprietary immune system management technology licensed from Yeda Research & Development Company Limited. The company's Veto Cell technology aims to induce sustained tolerance of transplanted cells in patients, enabling safer haploidentical stem cell transplants and allogeneic CAR-T therapy. The initial focus is on treating hematological malignancies (blood cancers) such as lymphoma, leukemia, and multiple myeloma.
sellsTo: Patients undergoing hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (HSCT)
Patients eligible for CAR-T cell therapy

exposures.sentence: The unique advantage of Cell Source technology lies in the ability to induce sustained tolerance of transplanted cells (or organs) by the recipient s immune system in a setting that requires only mild immune suppression, while avoiding the most common transplant related complications.

catalysts.event: Completion of first five treatment cohorts in Phase 1/2 clinical trial at MD Anderson
catalysts.sentence: The Phase 1/2 clinical trial at the University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center, using Cell Source s Anti-viral Veto Cells, has completed the first five treatment cohorts, with 15 patients each receiving a haploidentical HSCT under reduced intensity conditioning with Veto Cells.

catalysts.event: Submission of clinical protocol for allogeneic VETO CAR-T HSCT combined therapy for blood cancer treatment
catalysts.sentence: Cell Source plans to submit this protocol for approval in 2025.

tamClaims.claim: Global blood cancer therapeutics market estimated at $80 billion in 2025, projected to $112 billion by 2030
tamClaims.sentence: The global market for blood cancer therapeutics was estimated at $80 billion in 2025 and is projected to increase in size to reach $112 billion by 2030 according to The Business Research Company: Blood Cancer Drugs Market Report 2026 .
